plugins.name: github
plugins.description: GitHub integration via GitHub's official hosted MCP server — search repos / issues / PRs, read files, list commits. Connects over HTTPS, no Docker or local install. Requires a GitHub Personal Access Token.
plugins.author: GitHub (server) + Anybridge AI (catalog entry)

GITHUB_TOKEN.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) — needs read access to whatever you want the agent to see. Create one at https://github.com/settings/tokens

plugins.name: memory
plugins.description: Persistent knowledge graph for the agent — stores entities, relations, and observations across sessions via the official MCP memory server. No external auth required.
plugins.author: Anthropic (MCP) + Anybridge AI (catalog entry)

plugins.name: superpowers
plugins.description: 14 composable workflow skills (TDD, systematic debugging, brainstorming, writing/executing plans, subagent-driven development, git worktrees, code review) plus a session-start bootstrap. No MCP servers — installs a skill tree that the agent auto-loads on relevant tasks.
plugins.author: Jesse Vincent (obra)

plugins.name: kubectl
plugins.description: Kubernetes cluster access via the containers/kubernetes-mcp-server — list/inspect/apply resources, view logs, run pods. Native Go client (no kubectl binary required in PATH). Uses your existing kubeconfig.
plugins.author: containers org (server) + Anybridge AI (catalog entry)

KUBECONFIG.description: Path to kubeconfig file (must exist on disk). Typical value: /home/<you>/.kube/config
